title: Dark Room
prompt: Why can we not see in a dark cupboard?

options: No light to reflect into eyes

explanation: We need light sources. Light reflects off objects into our eyes so we see. Darkness is absence of light.
funFact: Cats can see in much dimmer light than us — but not total darkness either!

title: Shadow Size
prompt: Move torch closer to hand puppet — what happens to shadow size?
explanation: Closer light source makes bigger shadow because light is blocked at wider angle.
funFact: Your shadow at noon is shortest because sun is highest!

title: Mirror Maze
explanation: Shiny smooth surfaces reflect well. Dark rough surfaces absorb light.
funFact: The Moon shines because it reflects sunlight — it makes no light of its own!

title: Sun Safety
prompt: What should you never do with bright sunlight?
explanation: Sunlight can damage eyes. Never look directly at sun even with sunglasses.
funFact: Astronauts wear gold visors to protect eyes from harsh sunlight in space!

title: How Sound Made
explanation: Vibrations make sound. Bigger vibrations make louder sound. Vibrations travel through air water or solid to our ears.
funFact: Put rice on a drum and hit it — watch rice jump from vibrations!

title: High Low Pitch
prompt: Short ruler over desk edge plucked — high or low pitch?
explanation: Shorter, tighter, thinner vibrate faster giving higher pitch. Longer slower gives lower pitch.
funFact: A guitar uses thick strings for low notes and thin strings for high notes!

title: Loudness Test
explanation: Gentler hit makes smaller vibrations so quieter volume. Further away also quieter.
funFact: Sound cannot travel in space because there is no air — total silence!

title: String Telephone
prompt: String telephone works best when string is...
explanation: Vibrations travel better through tight solid string than loose floppy string or air alone.
funFact: Whales can hear each other hundreds of kilometres away underwater because sound travels well in water!

title: Quiet Room
prompt: Which material best muffles sound for insulation?
explanation: Soft thick materials absorb vibrations and muffle sound. Hard thin materials let sound through or reflect.
funFact: Recording studios use egg-box shaped foam to trap sound echoes!
